title: Setting the Forge
description:
Setup the perfect Windows 11 Dev Environment.

• WSL2 + Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
• VSCode / PyCharm with optimal extensions (Pylance, Ruff, GitLens, REST Client)
• Git configuration, SSH keys, GPG signing
• Python 3.12 via pyenv, virtualenv workflow
• PostgreSQL 16, Redis, Docker Desktop
• Windows Terminal with Starship prompt
• Automate with a dotfiles repository on GitHub
hardcore_element: Configure a custom Starship prompt showing git status, Python version, and active virtualenv. Commit your dotfiles to a public GitHub repo. Time yourself: can you spin up a fresh Django dev environment in under 10 minutes?
definition_of_done:
✓ Full Django + PostgreSQL + Redis stack spins up from scratch in < 10 minutes
✓ Dotfiles repo public on GitHub
✓ Custom terminal prompt configured

title: DSA Initial Assessment (Codeforces Bronze)
description:
Establish your baseline. Solve 50 Codeforces problems rated 1000–1200 focused on implementation, basic math, simulation, and greedy.

• Problems: A/B from Div. 3, Div. 4 rounds
• Topics: modular arithmetic, simple arrays, string manipulation, brute force
• Review the entire USACO Guide Bronze section
hardcore_element: Attempt a full live Codeforces Div. 3 round. For every problem you fail, solve it within 24 hours and perform an in-depth post-mortem analysis.
definition_of_done:
✓ 50 Codeforces problems solved (1000–1200 range)
✓ USACO Bronze guide completely reviewed
✓ Comfortably solving 3 problems within a Div. 3 time limit

title: Python Core Reinforcement
description:
Python fundamentals at expert level.

• Built-in types: list, dict, set, tuple, frozenset
• Comprehensions: list, dict, set, generator
• Built-in functions: map, filter, zip, enumerate, sorted, any, all
• Standard libraries: collections (Counter, defaultdict, deque), itertools, functools, heapq, bisect, math
• String internals and formatting
• File I/O and context managers
hardcore_element: Implement these 10 algorithms from memory with zero syntax errors: BFS, DFS (recursive + iterative), Binary Search, QuickSort, MergeSort, Dijkstra (basic), KMP String Search, Sieve of Eratosthenes, Union-Find (with path compression), Trie (insert/search).
definition_of_done:
✓ All 10 algorithms implemented from memory
✓ Any LeetCode Easy problem solved in under 10 minutes
✓ heapq, bisect, collections mastered with 5 practical examples each

title: Foundations of Complexity
description:
Master algorithmic analysis before writing a single line of competitive code.

• Big O, Big Omega, Big Theta notation
• Amortized analysis (dynamic array, hash table)
• Worst-case vs. average-case vs. best-case
• Python built-in complexity analysis: list.append O(1) amortized, dict lookup O(1) avg
• Recurrence relations (Master Theorem)
• Space complexity analysis
hardcore_element: Derive the time complexity of Python's TimSort from first principles. Explain why hash table average-case is O(1) by tracing Python's dict implementation.
definition_of_done:
✓ Can state complexity of any standard operation across all Python built-ins without hesitation
✓ Can back complexity claims with mathematical reasoning
✓ Solved 10 complexity-analysis exercises

title: Sorting & Searching (USACO Silver)
description:
Go beyond knowing sorting exists — understand every algorithm deeply.

• Binary Search: on sorted arrays, on answer (parametric search)
• Ternary Search: on unimodal functions
• QuickSort/MergeSort internals, Counting Sort, Radix Sort
• Python TimSort: custom sort keys, comparators via functools.cmp_to_key
• USACO Silver: binary search on answer pattern
hardcore_element: Implement Ternary Search to find minimum of f(x) = (x-3)² + 5 to 6 decimal places. Solve 5 USACO Silver problems requiring binary search on the answer.
definition_of_done:
✓ All sorting algorithms implemented from scratch
✓ 5 USACO Silver binary-search-on-answer problems solved
✓ Can identify "binary search on answer" pattern in novel problems

title: Linear Data Structures
description:
Build every structure from scratch — no built-ins allowed.

• Singly & Doubly Linked Lists (insert, delete, reverse, cycle detection)
• Stack: array-based and linked-list-based
• Queue: circular buffer implementation
• Deque: from scratch using circular buffer
• Monotonic Stack: Next Greater Element, Largest Rectangle in Histogram
• Monotonic Queue: Sliding Window Maximum
hardcore_element: Implement a full Deque from scratch using a circular buffer (O(1) all operations). Then solve "Sliding Window Maximum" using a Monotonic Deque. Solve 10 monotonic stack/queue problems.
definition_of_done:
✓ All structures implemented from scratch, tested with edge cases
✓ 10 monotonic stack/queue problems solved
✓ Can trace through Floyd's cycle detection algorithm for any linked list

title: Non-Linear Data Structures (Silver)
description:
Trees, heaps, and hash maps — the backbone of all advanced algorithms.

• Binary Search Trees: insert, delete, search, in-order traversal
• AVL/Red-Black Trees: conceptual understanding
• Binary Heap: min-heap and max-heap from scratch
• Hash Maps: chaining vs. open addressing, load factor, rehashing
• Trie (Prefix Tree): insert, search, startsWith, autocomplete
hardcore_element: Implement a min-heap from scratch: insert, extract_min, heapify_up, heapify_down. Implement a Trie supporting: insert, search, delete, autocomplete (returns all words with prefix).
definition_of_done:
✓ Binary Heap implemented from scratch with all operations
✓ Trie with autocomplete implemented
✓ 10 heap/trie problems solved

title: Prefix Sum & Two-Pointers Mastery
description:
The bread and butter of USACO Silver.

• 1D Prefix Sums: range sum queries in O(1)
• 2D Prefix Sums: rectangle sum queries in O(1)
• Difference Arrays: range update in O(1)
• Sliding Window: fixed and variable size
• Two Pointers: sorted array problems
• USACO Silver specific techniques: solving 30 problems
hardcore_element: Solve 30 USACO Silver problems using Prefix Sum and Two-Pointer techniques. Include at least 5 problems requiring 2D prefix sums.
definition_of_done:
✓ 30 USACO Silver problems solved
✓ 2D prefix sum formula derived from first principles
✓ Can solve any sliding window problem in O(n) without hints

title: Advanced Python: Under the Hood
description:
Move from Python user to Python expert.

• Generators & Iterators: __iter__/__next__, send(), yield from
• Decorators: functools.wraps, class-based, decorator factories
• Context Managers: __enter__/__exit__, contextlib
• Metaclasses: __new__ vs __init__, type(), custom metaclass applications
• Memory Management: reference counting, gc module, weak references, __slots__
• The GIL: what it is, why it exists, threading vs. multiprocessing
• asyncio: event loop, coroutines, Tasks, gather, Semaphore, Lock
hardcore_element: Write a @retry(max_attempts=3, backoff=2.0) decorator with exponential backoff. Build an async rate-limiter using asyncio.Semaphore that limits N concurrent calls.
definition_of_done:
✓ GIL implications explained for CPU-bound vs. I/O-bound
✓ @retry decorator built and tested
✓ Async rate-limiter verified with concurrent.futures test
✓ Built a working async scraper using aiohttp + asyncio

title: Django Architecture & Lifecycle
description:
Know Django so well you could debug any issue without documentation.

• The complete request-response cycle (URLconf → Middleware → View → Response)
• WSGI vs. ASGI: synchronous vs. asynchronous mode
• Middleware stack: process_request, process_response, process_view, process_exception
• Django settings: settings modules, django-environ, 12-factor app
• Template engine, static files handling, management commands
hardcore_element: Build a production-grade middleware suite: (1) Request Timing logging p95/p99 latencies, (2) Request-ID injection with UUID4, (3) IP Blacklist backed by database-driven blocklist with in-memory caching.
definition_of_done:
✓ Middleware suite fully implemented and unit-tested
✓ Can draw the complete Django request-response cycle from memory
✓ Understands WSGI vs ASGI with concrete use cases

title: Django ORM: The Battle of N+1
description:
DB query optimization separates mid from senior Django engineers.

• Queryset evaluation mechanics: lazy loading, _result_cache
• select_related vs prefetch_related (SQL JOINs vs separate queries)
• Prefetch objects with custom querysets
• annotate() vs aggregate(), F() and Q() expressions
• select_for_update(): pessimistic locking
• django-debug-toolbar: profiling in development
hardcore_element: Take a Django view that fires 100+ queries. Profile with django-debug-toolbar. Reduce to < 5 queries using ORM optimization. Document every change with before/after SQL analysis.
definition_of_done:
✓ 100-query view reduced to < 5 queries
✓ Can explain the exact SQL generated by any ORM call
✓ django-debug-toolbar profiling documented
✓ N+1 solved in 3 different scenarios

title: Domain-Driven Design in Django
description:
Architect Django apps that don't become unmaintainable at scale.

• Service Layer pattern: pure Python functions encapsulating business logic
• Selector pattern: DB query encapsulation separate from services
• Repository pattern: abstracting data access
• Django Signals: use cases and anti-patterns
• Testing strategy: 100% coverage on service layer
• Reference: HackSoftware's Django Styleguide
hardcore_element: Take an existing fat-model Django app. Refactor it to Service + Selector architecture. Write tests for the service layer to achieve 100% coverage using pytest-django.
definition_of_done:
✓ App fully refactored to Service/Selector architecture
✓ 100% test coverage on service layer verified with coverage.py
✓ Can articulate trade-offs between DDD patterns and simpler approaches

title: Django REST Framework Power User
description:
DRF deep mastery — every Senior Django JD mentions this.

• Serializer internals: validation flow, to_representation, to_internal_value
• ViewSets & Routers: ModelViewSet, CustomActions (@action)
• Custom Permissions & Authentication, JWT via SimpleJWT
• Throttling: AnonRateThrottle, UserRateThrottle, ScopedRateThrottle
• Pagination: PageNumberPagination, CursorPagination
• API Versioning strategies, drf-spectacular (OpenAPI 3.0)
hardcore_element: Build a paginated, versioned, JWT-authenticated REST API for a blog platform. Nested comments, like/unlike, user following, real-time validation. Document fully with drf-spectacular.
definition_of_done:
✓ Full blog API with all listed features
✓ OpenAPI 3.0 docs generated and accurate
✓ JWT + refresh token flow tested end-to-end
✓ All throttling and pagination strategies implemented

title: Graph Theory Fundamentals (Gold)
description:
Graphs are everywhere — social networks, routing, state machines.

• DFS/BFS (iterative + recursive), time/discovery stamps
• Connected Components, Bipartite Check, Flood Fill
• Topological Sort: Kahn's algorithm + DFS-based
• Strongly Connected Components: Kosaraju's, Tarjan's algorithm
• Cycle Detection in directed and undirected graphs
• USACO Gold: solving 10 graph problems
hardcore_element: Implement Tarjan's SCC algorithm from memory. Solve 10 USACO Gold graph problems: at least 3 TopSort, 3 BFS/DFS, 2 SCC, 2 Bipartite/Flood Fill.
definition_of_done:
✓ Tarjan's SCC implemented and tested
✓ 10 USACO Gold graph problems solved
✓ Can trace through Kosaraju's algorithm on paper

title: Shortest Paths & MST (Gold)
description:
Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, and MST algorithms mastered to implementation level.

• Dijkstra's: with min-heap O((V+E)logV), with matrix O(V²)
• Bellman-Ford: negative edge detection, SPFA
• Floyd-Warshall: all-pairs shortest path
• Kruskal's MST: with DSU, Prim's MST with priority queue
• Variants: shortest path with constraints, k-th shortest path
hardcore_element: Implement Dijkstra's from scratch using a custom binary min-heap (NOT heapq). Solve 5 USACO Gold shortest path or MST problems.
definition_of_done:
✓ Dijkstra's implemented with custom min-heap
✓ 5 USACO Gold shortest path problems solved
✓ Correctly selected algorithm for 10 different problem scenarios

title: Dynamic Programming (Gold)
description:
DP is the most asked topic in 40LPA+ technical interviews.

• Memoization vs. Tabulation
• Classic DPs: 0/1 Knapsack, Unbounded, Coin Change, LCS, LIS, Edit Distance
• Interval DP: Matrix Chain Multiplication, Burst Balloons
• Tree DP: Max independent set, tree diameter, rerooting
• Digit DP, Bitmask DP, DP on DAGs
• DP optimization: divide and conquer, convex hull trick (intro)
hardcore_element: Solve the Travelling Salesman Problem with Bitmask DP. Solve 20 USACO Gold DP problems including 3 Bitmask DPs, 3 Tree DPs, 3 Interval DPs.
definition_of_done:
✓ TSP solved with Bitmask DP
✓ 20 USACO Gold DP problems solved
✓ Can derive DP recurrences for novel problems without hints

title: Disjoint Set Union (Gold)
description:
Simple structure, profound applications.

• Basic DSU: find() and union() from scratch
• Path Compression: full, halving, splitting
• Union by Rank and Union by Size
• Amortized analysis: near-O(α(n)) per operation
• Weighted DSU: tracking relative values
• Rollback DSU: undo operations
• Applications: Kruskal's MST, offline connectivity queries
hardcore_element: Implement Weighted DSU for equivalence classes with weights. Solve 5 USACO Gold problems where DSU is the core data structure, including one offline problem.
definition_of_done:
✓ DSU with path compression + union by rank implemented
✓ Weighted DSU implemented and tested
✓ 5 USACO Gold DSU problems solved

title: Advanced Data Structures (Gold)
description:
Segment Trees and Fenwick Trees — the Swiss Army knives of competitive programming.

• Fenwick Tree / BIT: point update + prefix sum, range update + range query
• 2D Fenwick Tree
• Segment Tree: point update + range query (sum, min, max)
• Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation: range update + range query
• Sparse Table: static RMQ in O(1)
• Square Root Decomposition
hardcore_element: Implement a Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation from scratch (range add + range sum). Re-solve 3 problems using both Seg Tree and Fenwick Tree. Solve 5 USACO Gold problems using these structures.
definition_of_done:
✓ Segment Tree with Lazy Propagation fully implemented
✓ 5 USACO Gold problems solved using Seg Tree / BIT
✓ Sparse Table O(1) RMQ implemented

title: PostgreSQL Mastery
description:
Go far beyond basic SQL — this is what Staff engineers know.

• Advanced Indexing: B-Tree, Hash, GIN (JSONB/arrays), GiST (geometric/range), BRIN
• EXPLAIN ANALYZE: reading query plans, identifying seq scans
• Transaction Isolation Levels: Read Uncommitted → Serializable
• MVCC: how PostgreSQL implements isolation without read locks
• Partitioning: Range, List, Hash — with Django migrations
• Window Functions: ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG, LEAD, NTILE
• CTEs and Recursive CTEs, Full-Text Search
hardcore_element: Write a Django migration that introduces Range Partitioning to a 10M-row Orders table (partitioned by created_at month). Measure and document query speedup with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before/after.
definition_of_done:
✓ Partitioned 10M-row table, measured speedup documented
✓ Can explain MVCC with concurrency anomaly examples
✓ Written CTEs, Window Functions, and FTS queries in production code

title: Scalability Concepts
description:
The architectural vocabulary of 40LPA engineers.

• Caching strategies: Cache-Aside, Write-through, Write-back, Write-around
• Cache stampede prevention, Redis data structures (String, Hash, List, Set, Sorted Set, Stream)
• Load Balancing: Round Robin, Least Connections, Consistent Hashing
• Rate Limiting: Token Bucket, Leaky Bucket, Fixed/Sliding Window
• Database Read Replicas: Django multi-database router
• Connection Pooling: PgBouncer transaction mode
hardcore_element: Implement Redis Cache-Aside in Django for a high-read endpoint. Implement a Token Bucket rate limiter using Redis + Lua scripts for atomicity. Measure cache hit ratio under load.
definition_of_done:
✓ Cache-Aside with 95%+ hit ratio implemented and tested
✓ Token Bucket rate limiter with Lua scripts implemented
✓ Can draw full load balancing and caching architecture diagram

title: Distributed Systems Fundamentals
description:
Understanding why distributed systems are hard — and how to reason about them.

• CAP Theorem: Consistency vs. Availability under Partition
• PACELC Theorem, BASE properties, Eventual Consistency
• Real-world examples: Cassandra (AP), HBase (CP), DynamoDB (configurable)
• Idempotency: designing idempotent APIs with idempotency keys
• SAGA Pattern: choreography vs. orchestration, compensating transactions
• Raft conceptual understanding, Gossip Protocols, Consistent Hashing
hardcore_element: Design and implement a SAGA orchestrator: Order → Payment → Inventory → Shipping. If any step fails, rollback via compensating transactions. Implement using Django + Celery.
definition_of_done:
✓ SAGA orchestrator with compensating transactions implemented
✓ Can explain CAP theorem with real NoSQL database examples
✓ Designed idempotent payment API with idempotency key + DB uniqueness constraint

title: HLD System Case Studies
description:
Design canonical systems that appear in every 40LPA interview.

• Payment Gateway (Razorpay-style): idempotency, double-charge prevention, reconciliation
• Real-time Auction System: WebSocket scaling, bid locking, optimistic concurrency
• Social Network Feed (Instagram-style): fan-out on write vs. read, celebrity problem
• URL Shortener, Distributed Rate Limiter, Notification System
• LLD warmup: Splitwise, Parking Lot, Tic-Tac-Toe, Chess
hardcore_element: Complete the Splitwise Machine Coding challenge from scratch in Python in under 2 hours (full OOP, balance settlement, minimum transactions). Then present a full Razorpay-style payment gateway HLD.
definition_of_done:
✓ Splitwise completed in < 2 hours with minimum-transaction settlement
✓ Full system designs prepared for all 6 canonical systems
✓ Can identify bottlenecks and propose solutions in any of the above

title: Low-Level Design (LLD) Mastery
description:
Design patterns and SOLID principles — foundation of senior engineering.

• SOLID in Python: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP (beyond basic definitions)
• Creational: Factory Method, Abstract Factory, Builder, Singleton (thread-safe), Prototype
• Structural: Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade, Proxy, Flyweight
• Behavioral: Observer, Strategy, Command, Template Method, State, Chain of Responsibility
• Anti-patterns and when NOT to use patterns
hardcore_element: Implement a Plugin Architecture for a task processing engine: Factory to register/create plugins dynamically, Strategy pattern for interchangeable algorithms, Observer for lifecycle hooks, support hot-reloading without engine restart.
definition_of_done:
✓ Plugin architecture with 3+ built-in plugins and hot-reload support
✓ Can identify and justify the correct design pattern for any problem
✓ 20 LLD problems solved/designed

title: Containerization (Docker)
description:
Docker is table stakes for any senior backend role.

• Docker internals: Linux namespaces, cgroups, union filesystems (OverlayFS)
• Optimized Dockerfiles: multi-stage builds, layer caching, .dockerignore
• Base image: python:3.12-slim vs alpine trade-offs, non-root user
• Docker Compose: Django + Gunicorn + Nginx + PostgreSQL + Redis + Celery + Flower
• Health checks, Docker networking, Volumes vs Bind Mounts
hardcore_element: Write a production-grade docker-compose.yml with all 8 services. All must pass health checks. Django must serve through Nginx with gzip and static file caching. Multi-stage Dockerfile < 200MB.
definition_of_done:
✓ Full Compose stack running with all health checks passing
✓ Django serving through Nginx with proper headers
✓ Multi-stage Dockerfile < 200MB
✓ Can explain namespace and cgroup isolation

title: Kubernetes Orchestration
description:
K8s is the operating system for the cloud. Know it deeply.

• K8s Architecture: Control Plane (API Server, etcd, Scheduler), Node components (kubelet, kube-proxy)
• Primitives: Pods, ReplicaSets, Deployments, StatefulSets, Jobs, CronJobs
• Services: ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, Ingress (Nginx, TLS)
• ConfigMaps, Secrets, PersistentVolumes, HPA
• Resource requests/limits, Rolling updates, Helm charts
hardcore_element: Deploy full stack to Minikube/Kind. Configure HPA for Django pods (scale when CPU > 70%). Simulate load with k6 and verify auto-scaling. Implement a Helm chart for the entire application.
definition_of_done:
✓ Full stack deployed to local K8s
✓ HPA configured and verified under load test
✓ Helm chart created and working
✓ Explains Deployment vs StatefulSet for PostgreSQL in production

title: Message Queues & Streaming (Kafka)
description:
Async communication is the heart of scalable microservices.

• Messaging: Pub/Sub vs. Point-to-Point Queue
• Kafka internals: Topics, Partitions, Offsets, Consumer Groups, Brokers
• Delivery semantics: At-most-once, At-least-once, Exactly-once
• Dead Letter Queues, RabbitMQ vs Kafka comparison
• Celery: task queues, beat scheduler, result backends, chord/chain/group
hardcore_element: Implement a notification service: Django publishes events to Kafka → Celery consumer routes to email/push/SMS based on user preferences → failed notifications go to DLQ with retry logic + Flower monitoring.
definition_of_done:
✓ Full Kafka → Celery notification pipeline implemented
✓ DLQ with retry logic tested with fault injection
✓ Can explain Kafka consumer group rebalancing from memory

title: CI/CD Pipelines
description:
Automate everything. Ship with confidence. Sleep at night.

• GitHub Actions: workflow syntax, triggers, jobs, matrix builds
• Django pipeline: lint (ruff) → type check (mypy) → tests (pytest) → coverage gate
• Docker image build/push to GHCR, zero-downtime deployment strategies
• Secrets management (GitHub Secrets), pre-commit hooks
• GitLab CI/CD basics
hardcore_element: Build a complete GitHub Actions pipeline: lint → test (coverage ≥ 80%) → Docker build → push to GHCR → deploy to staging on PR merge → production deploy on main with manual approval gate.
definition_of_done:
✓ Full pipeline runs on every PR
✓ Coverage gate enforced at ≥ 80%
✓ Docker image pushed to GHCR automatically
✓ Staging auto-deploy on PR merge working

title: Observability & Monitoring
description:
You can't fix what you can't see. The three pillars of observability.

• Metrics: Prometheus data model, PromQL, django-prometheus
• Grafana: dashboards, variables, alerting rules
• The Four Golden Signals: Latency, Traffic, Errors, Saturation
• Logs: structured logging (structlog), ELK Stack, or Loki + Promtail
• Traces: OpenTelemetry, Jaeger/Tempo (distributed tracing)
• SLO/SLI/SLA: error budgets, burn rate alerts
hardcore_element: Deploy complete observability stack via docker-compose. Create Grafana dashboards: request rate, error rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, DB query time, cache hit ratio. Configure alert: error rate > 1% for > 5 minutes.
definition_of_done:
✓ Prometheus + Grafana stack deployed
✓ Four Golden Signals visible in Grafana
✓ Alert for error rate > 1% configured and tested
✓ Structured logging sending to ELK/Loki

title: Security Hardening
description:
Security is non-negotiable at 40LPA companies. Know OWASP Top 10 cold.

• Injection: SQL, NoSQL, command — Django-specific mitigations
• Broken Authentication: session management, JWT security (alg:none attack)
• XSS: reflected, stored, DOM-based — Django auto-escaping, CSP headers
• CSRF: Django CSRF middleware internals, SameSite cookies
• IDOR, Security Misconfiguration, SSRF, OAuth2 security (state, PKCE)
hardcore_element: Audit your Django project with Bandit (static analysis) + Safety (dependency vulns). Fix ALL high/medium findings. Implement strict Content Security Policy via middleware. Demonstrate JWT alg:none attack defense.
definition_of_done:
✓ Zero high/medium Bandit findings
✓ Zero known vulnerable dependencies
✓ CSP middleware implemented and verified
✓ Can describe 8+ OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities with Django-specific examples

title: The Capstone: Final Assault on 40LPA
description:
Build, deploy, and optimize a production-grade distributed application.

• Domain: Real-time Crypto Analytics Engine
• Stack: Django ASGI + WebSockets (Channels), Kafka for transaction streaming, PostgreSQL with range partitioning, Redis for channel layers + caching, Celery for aggregation jobs, Kubernetes on AWS EKS, Nginx Ingress, Full observability, CI/CD pipeline
• Features: Live price dashboard (WebSocket), Historical analytics API, Alert system via Kafka + Celery, Portfolio tracking per user, Admin panel with metrics
• Architecture diagram with full data flow annotations
hardcore_element: Deploy to AWS EKS. Load test with Locust/k6: handle 10,000 requests per second. Profile and fix every bottleneck. Write a detailed technical blog post about architecture decisions, challenges, and measured results.
definition_of_done:
✓ Application deployed to AWS EKS
✓ Handles 10,000 RPS under sustained load test
✓ P99 latency < 200ms at peak load
✓ Full observability with dashboards
✓ CI/CD pipeline deploying on every merge
✓ Architecture blog post published

title: Resume & Brand Building (40LPA Specific)
description:
The technical work means nothing if you can't sell it.

• Quantify every impact: "Reduced API latency from 800ms to 90ms by Redis caching + DB optimization"
• Narrative: architecture decisions, scale solved, complexities overcome
• ATS optimization: keyword targeting for Senior/Staff Django roles
• LinkedIn: featured projects, skills (PostgreSQL, Django, Kubernetes, Kafka), recommendations
• GitHub profile: pinned repos, comprehensive READMEs, architecture diagrams
• Target: Razorpay, BrowserStack, Groww, Meesho, Rippling, Postman, Chargebee
hardcore_element: Write 3 technical blog posts (min 1500 words each) on your capstone, Kafka pipeline, or K8s deployment. Target 1,000 views on at least one post within 30 days using dev.to + LinkedIn.
definition_of_done:
✓ Resume quantified, reviewed by at least 1 senior engineer
✓ LinkedIn profile with 500+ connections and 3 featured projects
✓ 3 blog posts published
✓ Applied to 20 target companies with personalized applications

title: Mock Interviews & Negotiation Mastery
description:
The interview is a skill. Train like an athlete.

• System Design (HLD) mocks: Twitter, Uber, WhatsApp, Netflix (10 full sessions)
• LLD mocks: Parking Lot, Chess, Library Management, Snake & Ladder (10 sessions)
• DSA mock contests: 2-hour timed sessions, real interview pressure
• Behavioral: STAR format, leadership stories, conflict resolution
• Negotiation: base + ESOP + variable, competing offer strategy
• ESOP valuation: vesting schedules, cliff, strike price
• Notice period leveraging, understanding market rates at target companies
hardcore_element: Complete 10 mock HLD + 10 mock LLD sessions. Achieve average ≥ 8/10. Role-play: negotiate a mock offer from 30LPA to 42LPA using a competing offer.
definition_of_done:
✓ Average ≥ 8/10 across 20 mock sessions (logged in this app)
✓ Negotiation script prepared and rehearsed
✓ Can present any of 4 canonical HLD systems in 45 minutes with trade-off analysis
